meta.title: 1997 Volvo S90 — Build Tracker
meta.subtitle: B6304S 2.9L Inline-6 · RWD · 133,000 miles · AW30-43 4-Speed Auto

tasks.text: Timing belt replaced
tasks.note: Done at 133,800 miles. Next service due at ~203,800 miles or 7 years, whichever comes first.

tasks.text: Hydraulic belt tensioner replaced
tasks.note: Done at 133,800 miles.

tasks.text: Idler pulley / roller replaced
tasks.note: Done at 133,800 miles.

tasks.text: Tensioner roller replaced
tasks.note: Done at 133,800 miles.

tasks.text: Water pump replaced
tasks.note: Done at 133,800 miles.

tasks.text: Thermostat and housing
tasks.note: Quick standalone job — no belt removal needed. Stuck-open = runs cool and rich. Stuck-closed = overheating.

tasks.text: New rotors and pads all around
tasks.note: EBC Greenstuff pads recommended — solid daily-driver upgrade over stock.

tasks.text: Flush brake fluid with DOT 4
Inspect brake lines — replace with stainless steel braided lines
tasks.note: Better pedal feel and longevity. Inspect for cracking first.

tasks.text: Rebuild or replace calipers if sticking
Replace all coolant hoses (upper, lower, heater core, expansion tank hoses)
New expansion tank
tasks.note: Plastic becomes brittle over time — known failure point on this platform.

tasks.text: Drain and refill with Volvo-spec HOAT coolant (phosphate-free, 50/50 with distilled water)
tasks.note: Completed. Next coolant service in 3 years.

tasks.text: Inspect radiator — already replaced by previous owner
tasks.note: Radiator has been replaced. Not original. Confirmed during coolant drain and refill.

tasks.text: Standalone transmission fluid cooler
tasks.note: Separates ATF cooling from the radiator. Eliminates the risk of radiator barrier failure contaminating the transmission. Cheap insurance for a 500k-mile car.

tasks.text: Braided stainless steel fluid lines (coolant and trans cooler)
tasks.note: Better durability and heat resistance than rubber. Replace coolant hoses and trans cooler lines with braided stainless.

tasks.text: Spark plugs — NGK or Bosch Platinum, gapped to spec
Ignition coil pack
tasks.note: Replace if any misfires present.

tasks.text: Spark plug wires — Bougicord or OEM-quality set
New fuel filter (inline, under the car)
Fuel injector cleaning or replacement — Bosch OEM
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for leaks
Clean throttle body and idle air control valve
Replace all control arm bushings (front and rear)
New front struts and rear shocks
tasks.note: Bilstein B4 OEM-replacement or Bilstein HD for a slightly firmer ride.

tasks.text: New strut mounts and bump stops
Inspect and replace sway bar end links and bushings
New tie rod ends and ball joints (inspect first)
Alignment after all suspension work
Drain and refill transmission fluid — Dexron III / Mercon
Replace transmission filter and pan gasket
Inspect transmission mount — replace if cracked or sagging
PCV system — clean or replace PCV valve and hoses
tasks.note: Oil sludge prevention. Don't skip this.

tasks.text: Vacuum hoses — replace any cracked or brittle lines
tasks.note: Common cause of idle issues and check engine lights.

tasks.text: Motor mounts — replace if torn
All accessory / serpentine belts
Airbox modification / high-flow panel filter (K&N or equivalent drop-in)
Intake manifold inspection — clean carbon buildup, fresh gasket
Performance exhaust header / manifold (tubular steel or ported stock)
Cat-back exhaust — 2.5-inch mandrel-bent system
tasks.note: Magnaflow, Borla, or custom stainless. Don't go larger than 2.5" on a NA 2.9L.

tasks.text: High-flow sports catalyst (if emissions laws allow)
ECU tune — piggyback (Apexi S-AFC), Motronic 4.4 swap, or standalone (Megasquirt)
Fast road camshaft — ~10–15° more duration, mild profile for daily driving
tasks.note: Requires timing belt removal — plan accordingly. Single biggest mechanical mod at this stage.

tasks.text: Head porting and polishing — port matching, bowl blending, exhaust port polish
Larger valves (if available for B6304S — even 1mm helps flow)
Three-angle valve job
Ported / upgraded intake manifold
High-flow fuel injectors (~20% over stock capacity)
Upgraded in-tank fuel pump
Adjustable fuel pressure regulator
More aggressive camshaft (step up from Stage 1 cam)
Tubular equal-length exhaust header (if not done at Stage 1)
Standalone ECU (Megasquirt or Haltech) — mandatory at this stage
Dyno tune
Upgraded torque converter (higher stall speed)
Turbocharger — Garrett GT2860RS or similar (or Volvo TD04 donor unit)
Custom exhaust manifold with turbo flange (fabricated or sourced)
External wastegate — Tial 38mm or similar
Front-mount intercooler (FMIC) — ~24×12×3" core
Charge piping (aluminum / silicone)
Blow-off valve / bypass valve
Turbo oil feed and return lines
Turbo coolant lines
Forged lower-compression pistons (~8.5:1 CR)
tasks.note: Stock is ~10.7:1 — must come down for safe boost levels.

tasks.text: Forged connecting rods
Multi-layer steel (MLS) head gasket
ARP head studs
Engine balancing and blueprinting
High-flow injectors (550cc+ for 300 hp target)
High-flow fuel pump
Standalone ECU — Megasquirt / Haltech
Wideband O2 sensor and gauge
Boost controller (manual or electronic)
Rebuilt / strengthened AW30-43 automatic (upgraded clutch packs, shift kit)
tasks.note: OR manual swap — Getrag 260 5-speed from 960/S90 donor.

tasks.text: Upgraded differential / LSD — Quaife ATB
Upgraded aluminum radiator
Oil cooler — sandwich plate + remote cooler
PCV catch can
Coilovers — adjustable, 960/S90 platform
Upgraded sway bars — IPD kit for 960 platform
Polyurethane bushings throughout
Subframe reinforcement
Big brake kit — Brembo or Wilwood, braided lines, DOT 4 racing fluid
